title: Quiz: Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors
description: Test your understanding of eigenvalues, eigenvectors, and their role in quantum measurement

questions.question: If Av = 3v for non-zero vector v, what is v called?

options.text: An eigenvector of A with eigenvalue 3

questions.explanation: When Av = λv, v is an eigenvector with eigenvalue λ. Here λ = 3.

questions.question: What are the eigenvalues of the Pauli-Z matrix [[1,0],[0,-1]]?
questions.explanation: Z is diagonal, so eigenvalues are the diagonal entries: +1 and -1.

questions.question: Which equation do eigenvalues satisfy?
questions.explanation: The characteristic equation det(A - λI) = 0 gives the eigenvalues.

questions.question: Why must quantum observables be Hermitian matrices?

options.text: So they commute with all other matrices

questions.explanation: Hermitian matrices have real eigenvalues. Since measurement outcomes must be real numbers, observables must be Hermitian.

questions.question: Eigenvectors of a Hermitian matrix corresponding to different eigenvalues are always orthogonal.
questions.explanation: True! This is a fundamental property of Hermitian matrices, and it's why quantum measurement basis states are orthogonal.

questions.question: The sum of eigenvalues of a matrix equals its determinant.
questions.explanation: False! Sum of eigenvalues = trace, Product of eigenvalues = determinant.

questions.question: What is |0⟩ with respect to the Pauli-X matrix?
questions.explanation: X|0⟩ = |1⟩ ≠ λ|0⟩. The eigenvectors of X are |+⟩ and |-⟩, not |0⟩ and |1⟩.

questions.question: Which are true about spectral decomposition A = Σλᵢ|ψᵢ⟩⟨ψᵢ|? (Select all)
questions.explanation: a-c are all correct. d is false - spectral decomposition works for Hermitian matrices of any size.
